description: A migraine is characterized by severe, throbbing pain, often on one side of the head, and can be accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light or sound.
advice: Take painkillers like paracetamol or ibuprofen and rest in a dark, quiet room.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have a bad throbbing pain at the front or side of your head, feel sick, or find light or noise painful.

description: Cluster headaches are extremely painful and occur in cyclical patterns or clusters, often causing intense pain around one eye.
advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you experience severe pain around the eye.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have severe headaches that occur in clusters and are accompanied by other symptoms like nasal congestion.

description: Tension headaches are the most common type, often caused by stress, poor posture, or eye strain, resulting in a dull, aching sensation.
advice: Try to relax, drink plenty of water, and take over-the-counter painkillers if necessary.
when_to_see_doctor: If your headache keeps coming back or painkillers do not help.

description: Hormonal headaches can occur due to fluctuations in hormones, often linked to menstrual cycles or menopause.
advice: Keep a headache diary to track patterns and consult a doctor for management options.
when_to_see_doctor: If you regularly get headaches before or during your period.

description: Meningitis can cause severe headaches along with other symptoms like fever and a stiff neck, indicating inflammation of the protective membranes covering the brain.
advice: Seek urgent medical attention if you experience a severe headache with high fever and symptoms of meningitis.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have a headache that comes on suddenly and is extremely painful, accompanied by confusion or loss of vision.

description: A cough often accompanies a common cold, which is caused by viral infections and usually resolves within a few weeks.
advice: Rest, drink plenty of fluids, and consider using over-the-counter pain relievers like paracetamol or ibuprofen.
when_to_see_doctor: See a GP if you've had a cough for more than 3 weeks or if you're losing weight for no reason.

description: Bronchitis can cause a persistent cough due to inflammation of the bronchial tubes, often following a cold or flu.
advice: Stay hydrated and rest; consider consulting a pharmacist for cough syrups or decongestants.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek urgent medical attention if your cough worsens, you have chest pain, or you're coughing up blood.

description: A cough can be a symptom of COVID-19, which may present with other symptoms such as fever and loss of taste or smell.
advice: Isolate yourself and get tested for COVID-19; monitor your symptoms closely.
when_to_see_doctor: Contact a doctor if you experience severe symptoms like difficulty breathing or persistent chest pain.

description: Coughing can result from allergies, such as hay fever, which irritate the throat and airways.
advice: Avoid allergens and consider using antihistamines to alleviate symptoms.
when_to_see_doctor: Consult a GP if you have persistent symptoms or if your cough is accompanied by other severe allergic reactions.

description: Coughing can be a symptom of acid reflux, where stomach acid irritates the throat and causes discomfort.
advice: Avoid large meals and lying down after eating; consider over-the-counter antacids.
when_to_see_doctor: See a doctor if you have frequent coughs along with heartburn or if your symptoms worsen.

description: A cold is a common viral infection that can cause a high temperature as the body fights off the virus.
advice: Get plenty of rest and drink fluids to stay hydrated.
when_to_see_doctor: If your fever persists or worsens despite home treatment.

description: The flu is a viral infection that can lead to a high temperature along with other symptoms like chills and body aches.
advice: Take paracetamol or ibuprofen if you feel uncomfortable and stay hydrated.
when_to_see_doctor: If your symptoms do not improve or if you experience severe symptoms.

description: A high temperature can indicate an infection as the body responds to pathogens.
advice: Rest and drink plenty of fluids; consider over-the-counter medications for discomfort.
when_to_see_doctor: If the fever is very high or does not decrease with treatment.

description: Fever can lead to dehydration, especially if accompanied by sweating or not drinking enough fluids.
advice: Ensure you drink enough fluids to keep your urine light yellow and clear.
when_to_see_doctor: If you feel dizzy, confused, or have very dark urine.

description: In some cases, a high temperature may signal a more serious underlying condition.
advice: Monitor your symptoms closely and seek medical advice if they worsen.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have a high temperature that is not improving or if you have other concerning symptoms.

description: Sleep apnoea can cause tiredness during the day due to interrupted sleep at night, often characterized by gasping or snoring.
advice: Seek medical evaluation if you experience loud snoring or gasping during sleep.
when_to_see_doctor: If you notice loud snoring or choking noises during sleep, or if your fatigue is affecting your daily life.

description: Iron deficiency anaemia can lead to fatigue, palpitations, and pale skin due to insufficient red blood cells.
advice: Consider dietary changes to include iron-rich foods and consult a doctor for testing.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience fatigue along with shortness of breath or noticeable heartbeats.

description: Diabetes may cause extreme fatigue along with increased thirst and frequent urination due to high blood sugar levels.
advice: Monitor your symptoms and maintain a healthy diet; consult a healthcare provider for blood sugar testing.
when_to_see_doctor: If you feel excessively tired and experience increased thirst or weight loss.

description: Hyperthyroidism can lead to constant fatigue, anxiety, and muscle weakness due to an overactive metabolism.
advice: Seek medical advice for thyroid function tests if you experience persistent fatigue and anxiety.
when_to_see_doctor: If you feel fatigued and have symptoms like nervousness or irritability.

possible_condition: Myalgic encephalomyelitis or chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS)
description: ME/CFS is characterized by extreme fatigue lasting more than six months, affecting daily activities and cognitive function.
advice: Consult a healthcare professional for a proper diagnosis and management plan.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience extreme fatigue for more than three months along with cognitive difficulties.

description: Norovirus or food poisoning can cause nausea along with symptoms like diarrhea or vomiting.
advice: Stay hydrated and rest; consider seeing a GP if symptoms persist.
when_to_see_doctor: If you are feeling sick and do not feel better in a few days or if you experience severe symptoms.

description: Acid reflux can lead to nausea, often accompanied by heartburn or bloating after eating.
advice: Avoid trigger foods and consider smaller, more frequent meals.
when_to_see_doctor: If nausea occurs frequently or is accompanied by severe discomfort.

description: Migraines can cause nausea, along with headaches and sensitivity to light or sound.
advice: Rest in a dark, quiet room and stay hydrated.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience frequent migraines or if nausea is severe.

description: Labyrinthitis or vertigo can cause dizziness and nausea, often related to balance issues.
advice: Avoid sudden movements and stay hydrated.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness is severe or persistent, or if accompanied by other concerning symptoms.

description: Morning sickness is common in pregnancy and can cause nausea, especially in the first trimester.
advice: Eat small, frequent meals and stay hydrated; ginger may help.
when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is severe or persistent, affecting daily activities.

description: Trapped wind can cause discomfort and pain in the stomach, often accompanied by bloating and excessive gas.
advice: Try to move around to help release the trapped gas and consider over-the-counter remedies.
when_to_see_doctor: If the pain persists or is severe, or if you experience significant bloating that does not go away.

description: Indigestion can lead to a feeling of fullness, bloating, and discomfort in the stomach after eating.
advice: Eat smaller meals and avoid spicy or fatty foods; consider antacids if necessary.
when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a few days or worsen over time.

description: Constipation can cause abdominal pain and discomfort due to difficulty in passing stools.
advice: Increase fiber intake and drink plenty of water; consider a mild laxative if needed.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have not had a bowel movement for several days or experience severe pain.

description: A stomach bug or food poisoning can cause pain, watery stools, nausea, and vomiting.
advice: Stay hydrated and rest; seek medical advice if symptoms are severe or persist.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have persistent vomiting, high fever, or signs of dehydration.

description: Appendicitis is characterized by sudden pain in the lower right abdomen and requires immediate medical attention.
advice: Do not eat or drink anything and seek emergency medical help immediately.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience sudden, severe abdominal pain, especially in the lower right side.

description: A viral infection is a common cause of sore throats, often accompanied by other symptoms like a runny nose or cough.
advice: Drink plenty of water and rest; consider gargling with warm salt water.
when_to_see_doctor: If the sore throat does not improve after a week or if you experience a high fever.

description: A bacterial infection, such as strep throat, can cause a severe sore throat and may require antibiotics.
advice: Consult a pharmacist or GP if you suspect a bacterial infection.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have a sore throat with a lump in your neck or mouth ulcers lasting more than 3 weeks.

description: Allergies can cause throat irritation and soreness, often accompanied by sneezing or itchy eyes.
advice: Avoid allergens and consider taking antihistamines if necessary.
when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist or worsen despite avoiding allergens.

description: GERD can cause acid to irritate the throat, leading to soreness and discomfort.
advice: Avoid spicy foods and eat smaller meals; consider over-the-counter antacids.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience frequent heartburn or if the sore throat persists.

description: Smoking or exposure to smoke can irritate the throat, causing soreness.
advice: Avoid smoking and smoky environments; stay hydrated.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have persistent soreness or other respiratory symptoms.

description: Vertigo is a sensation of spinning or feeling off-balance, often associated with inner-ear problems.
advice: Lie down until the dizziness passes, then get up slowly.
when_to_see_doctor: If the dizziness keeps coming back or is accompanied by hearing changes.

description: Dehydration can cause dizziness due to a lack of fluids in the body, leading to low blood pressure.
advice: Drink plenty of fluids, especially water.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness persists despite hydration or if you experience fainting.

description: Labyrinthitis is an inner-ear infection that can cause dizziness and balance issues, often following a cold or flu.
advice: Rest and avoid sudden movements until symptoms improve.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness is severe or accompanied by hearing loss.

description: Postural hypotension occurs when standing up suddenly, causing a drop in blood pressure and resulting in dizziness.
advice: Move slowly and carefully, especially when changing positions.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness occurs frequently or is associated with fainting.

description: Certain medications can cause dizziness as a side effect, impacting balance and coordination.
advice: Consult with your GP about any new medications that may be causing dizziness.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness starts after beginning a new medication or if it worsens.

description: Tension headaches are the most common type of headache among adults and teens, causing mild to moderate pain that can come and go over time.
advice: Try relaxation techniques, over-the-counter pain relievers, and ensure you are well-hydrated.
when_to_see_doctor: If headaches become more frequent or severe, or if they interfere with daily activities.

description: Migraine headaches are characterized by pounding, throbbing pain that can last from 4 hours to 3 days, often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light.
advice: Consider keeping a headache diary, avoiding known triggers, and consult a doctor for potential preventive treatments.
when_to_see_doctor: If migraines occur more than once a month or if the pain is unusually severe.

description: Cluster headaches are extremely painful and occur in cyclical patterns, often causing intense burning or piercing pain around one eye.
advice: Seek immediate medical attention for severe pain and consider treatments such as oxygen therapy or prescription medications.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience severe headaches that disrupt your daily life or if they occur in clusters.

description: Sinus headaches are associated with sinus infections and cause pain in the forehead, cheeks, and around the eyes, often accompanied by nasal congestion.
advice: Use decongestants, warm compresses, and stay hydrated to alleviate symptoms.
when_to_see_doctor: If headaches persist despite treatment or are accompanied by high fever or vision changes.

description: Rebound headaches occur from the overuse of pain medications, leading to a cycle of headaches that can worsen with continued use.
advice: Limit the use of over-the-counter pain medications and consult a doctor for a proper treatment plan.
when_to_see_doctor: If headaches worsen or if you find yourself taking pain medications more than twice a week.

description: COVID-19 is a viral infection that can cause a fever as one of its primary symptoms, indicating the body's response to the virus.
advice: Monitor your symptoms and stay hydrated. Rest and take over-the-counter medications if necessary.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience difficulty breathing, persistent chest pain, confusion, or bluish lips or face.

description: Influenza, or the flu, is a contagious respiratory illness that often presents with a fever as the body fights off the virus.
advice: Rest, stay hydrated, and consider antiviral medications if prescribed by a doctor.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have a high fever that lasts more than three days or if you experience severe symptoms.

description: Bacterial infections can lead to fever as the immune system responds to the invading bacteria.
advice: Seek medical attention for proper diagnosis and treatment, which may include antibiotics.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have a fever that is very high or persistent, or if you develop other severe symptoms.

description: Heat exhaustion can occur when the body overheats, leading to a fever-like temperature as a response.
advice: Move to a cooler place, hydrate, and rest. Seek medical help if symptoms worsen.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience confusion, fainting, or a high body temperature that does not decrease.

description: Certain autoimmune disorders can cause fever as the immune system mistakenly attacks healthy cells, leading to inflammation.
advice: Consult a healthcare provider for a thorough evaluation and management plan.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have a persistent fever accompanied by unexplained symptoms or worsening health.

description: A common viral infection that affects the upper respiratory tract, often leading to a cough as a symptom.
advice: Stay hydrated and rest; over-the-counter medications may help alleviate symptoms.
when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms worsen or persist beyond 10 days, or if you experience a high fever.

description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses, often accompanied by a cough, fever, and body aches.
advice: Rest, drink fluids, and consider antiviral medications if diagnosed early.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have difficulty breathing, chest pain, or a high fever that doesn't respond to medication.

description: A long-term inflammation of the bronchial tubes, leading to a persistent cough that produces mucus.
advice: Avoid irritants like smoke and consider bronchodilator medications.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience a cough that lasts more than eight weeks or has blood in it.

description: A chronic condition that causes inflammation and narrowing of the airways, often resulting in a cough, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
advice: Use prescribed inhalers and avoid known triggers.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have frequent coughing episodes or if your symptoms worsen.

description: An allergic reaction that can cause nasal congestion and postnasal drip, leading to a cough.
advice: Avoid allergens and consider antihistamines for relief.
when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms are severe or do not improve with over-the-counter treatments.

description: Anemia is a condition where you lack enough healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to your body's tissues, leading to fatigue.
advice: Consider increasing iron-rich foods in your diet and consult a healthcare provider for testing.
when_to_see_doctor: If fatigue persists despite adequate sleep or if you experience shortness of breath or unusual paleness.

description: Depression can manifest as persistent fatigue, where individuals feel exhausted even without physical exertion.
advice: Seek support from a mental health professional and consider therapy or medication.
when_to_see_doctor: If feelings of sadness or hopelessness accompany fatigue, or if you have thoughts of self-harm.

description: Sleep apnea is a disorder characterized by interrupted breathing during sleep, leading to excessive daytime fatigue.
advice: Consult a sleep specialist for evaluation and possible sleep study.
when_to_see_doctor: If you snore loudly, experience gasping during sleep, or feel excessively sleepy during the day.

description: Hypothyroidism occurs when the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough hormones, leading to fatigue and low energy levels.
advice: Get a thyroid function test and discuss treatment options with your doctor.
when_to_see_doctor: If you notice weight gain, sensitivity to cold, or changes in skin and hair along with fatigue.

description: Fibromyalgia is a chronic condition characterized by widespread pain and fatigue, often without a clear cause.
advice: Consider lifestyle changes, such as regular exercise and stress management techniques, and consult a healthcare provider.
when_to_see_doctor: If fatigue is accompanied by widespread pain or cognitive difficulties.

description: Gallbladder disease can cause nausea due to inflammation or blockage affecting digestion.
advice: Monitor your symptoms and consider dietary changes to reduce fat intake.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical attention if nausea is accompanied by severe abdominal pain or jaundice.

description: Food poisoning often presents with nausea as the body reacts to harmful bacteria or toxins.
advice: Stay hydrated and rest; avoid solid foods until nausea subsides.
when_to_see_doctor: Contact a doctor if symptoms persist for more than 24 hours or if you experience high fever.

description: Motion sickness occurs when there is a conflict between the senses, leading to nausea during travel.
advice: Try to focus on a stable point in the distance and consider over-the-counter medications.
when_to_see_doctor: Consult a doctor if motion sickness is frequent and disrupts daily activities.

description: Nausea is common in early pregnancy, often referred to as morning sickness.
advice: Eat small, frequent meals and stay hydrated; ginger may help alleviate symptoms.
when_to_see_doctor: Contact your healthcare provider if nausea is severe or prevents you from eating or drinking.

description: GERD can cause nausea due to stomach acid backing up into the esophagus.
advice: Avoid trigger foods and eat smaller meals; consider elevating your head while sleeping.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical advice if nausea is persistent or accompanied by chest pain.

description: Appendicitis is an inflammation of the appendix, often causing sudden and severe abdominal pain, particularly in the lower right abdomen.
advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect appendicitis.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience sudden, severe abdominal pain, especially if it's localized in the lower right abdomen.

description: Crohn's disease is a type of inflammatory bowel disease that can cause chronic abdominal pain, often accompanied by diarrhea and weight loss.
advice: Consult a healthcare provider for a proper diagnosis and treatment plan.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have persistent abdominal pain lasting more than three months, or if it is accompanied by other symptoms like diarrhea.

description: Kidney stones can cause severe colicky abdominal pain that comes in waves, often radiating to the back or groin.
advice: Drink plenty of fluids and consult a doctor if you suspect kidney stones.
when_to_see_doctor: If you experience severe pain that comes and goes, especially if accompanied by blood in urine.

description: Indigestion, or dyspepsia, can cause discomfort or pain in the stomach area, often related to eating.
advice: Try over-the-counter antacids and avoid trigger foods.
when_to_see_doctor: If the pain persists or worsens, or if you experience severe symptoms like vomiting or weight loss.

description: Gastroenteritis, often caused by a virus, leads to inflammation of the stomach and intestines, resulting in abdominal pain, diarrhea, and vomiting.
advice: Stay hydrated and rest; consult a doctor if symptoms are severe or persist.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have severe abdominal pain, high fever, or signs of dehydration.

description: Acute pharyngitis is a sudden inflammation of the pharynx, often caused by viral infections like the cold or flu, leading to a sore throat.
advice: Stay hydrated, rest, and consider over-the-counter pain relievers.
when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a week or worsen, seek medical attention.

description: Chronic pharyngitis refers to a sore throat that lasts longer than a few weeks, often due to ongoing irritants or infections.
advice: Identify and avoid irritants, and consult a healthcare provider for further evaluation.
when_to_see_doctor: If the sore throat is persistent and accompanied by other symptoms like weight loss or difficulty breathing.

description: Tonsillitis is the inflammation of the tonsils, which can cause a severe sore throat, especially when swallowing.
advice: Gargle with warm salt water and take pain relievers to ease discomfort.
when_to_see_doctor: If you notice white spots on your tonsils or have difficulty breathing.

description: Laryngitis is the inflammation of the larynx, which can lead to a sore throat and hoarseness.
advice: Rest your voice and stay hydrated; avoid irritants like smoke.
when_to_see_doctor: If hoarseness lasts more than two weeks or is accompanied by a high fever.

description: Epiglottitis is a serious bacterial infection causing swelling of the epiglottis, which can block airflow.
advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you experience difficulty breathing or swallowing.
when_to_see_doctor: If you have severe throat pain and difficulty swallowing or breathing.

description: BPPV is a common inner ear disorder where tiny bits of calcium in the inner ear become dislodged, causing brief episodes of dizziness, especially when tilting or turning the head.
advice: If you experience dizziness when moving your head, consider performing specific head exercises to reposition the calcium particles, and consult your doctor for guidance.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness persists or worsens, or if you experience additional symptoms like severe headache or loss of balance.

description: Cerebellar stroke occurs due to a blockage or rupture of blood vessels in the cerebellum, leading to dizziness, headache, and potential loss of vision or slurred speech.
advice: Seek emergency medical help immediately if you experience dizziness along with severe headache, vision changes, or facial drooping.
when_to_see_doctor: Any sudden onset of dizziness accompanied by neurological symptoms such as weakness, confusion, or difficulty speaking.

description: An inner ear infection can disrupt balance and cause dizziness, often accompanied by symptoms like ear pain or hearing loss.
advice: Consult a healthcare provider for appropriate treatment if you suspect an inner ear infection.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness is persistent and accompanied by ear pain, fever, or hearing changes.

description: Low blood pressure can lead to dizziness, especially when standing up quickly, due to inadequate blood flow to the brain.
advice: Stay hydrated and rise slowly from sitting or lying positions to minimize dizziness.
when_to_see_doctor: If you frequently experience dizziness upon standing or if it is accompanied by fainting or confusion.

description: Dehydration can lead to dizziness as it affects blood volume and circulation, causing reduced blood flow to the brain.
advice: Increase fluid intake, especially in hot weather or after exercise, to prevent dehydration-related dizziness.
when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness persists despite rehydration or is accompanied by severe thirst, dry mouth, or dark urine.
